---
title: "More about me..."
---

## Education

I have built an academic and professional career at the intersection of public health, spatial analysis, and social research, with a particular focus on health systems and maternal healthcare in low- and middle-income settings. I completed a Diploma in Health Information Management at the College of Health in Kintampo, graduating as the Best Student. I went on to earn a First-Class Bachelor of Public Health degree from the University of Ghana where I built a strong foundation for my later work in health data, policy, and research.

Driven by a commitment to producing actionable insights for health management and policy, I pursued postgraduate studies at the University of Southampton. There, I completed two master’s degrees with distinction. The first was in Applied GIS and Remote Sensing and another in Social Research Methods. Following the masters degrees, I completed a PhD in Human Geography, specialising in spatial epidemiology. My doctoral thesis, *Spatial patterns of birthing care utilisation in Ghana*, reflects my longstanding interest in the intersection of health services, spatial inequalities, and maternal outcomes. I later enhanced my teaching practice by completing a Postgraduate Certificate in Academic Practice and becoming a Fellow of the Higher Education Academy.

## Work Experience

My professional experience spans research, teaching, monitoring and evaluation, and public health practice. I currently serve as a Lecturer in Geographies of Health at University College London, following previous appointments at the University of Southampton as a Senior Research Fellow, Research Fellow, and Senior Research Assistant. Prior to my academic career in the UK, I worked within the Ghana Health Service, serving as a Public Health Officer at Kibi Government Hospital and as the District Health Information Officer for the Atiwa District. I also led monitoring and evaluation work for Good Neighbors Ghana, where I contributed to programme effectiveness and community-based health interventions.

## Impact and Awards

My contributions to research and impact have been recognised through several awards. I am the winner of the South Coast Doctoral Training Partnership ESRC Impact Prize and the runner-up for the National ESRC Early Career Impact Prize in the UK. I have also received the Doctoral College Director’s Award for Engagement and Impact. My academic achievements have been supported through competitive funding, including an Economic and Social Research Council 1+3 scholarship and a Commonwealth Scholarship to study at the University of Southampton. During my postgraduate studies, I was honoured four times times with the Dean’s List award for outstanding academic performance.

Across academia and public health practice, I remain committed to producing high-quality research, driving real-world impact, and supporting the next generation of scholars and practitioners in global health and geospatial analysis.
